conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Content Constraints
seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: The email must include specific reasons for my resignation, such as career growth opportunities or personal circumstances.
new_constraint: The phrase "I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning" must appear at least once in the email.
instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. The email must include specific reasons for my resignation, such as career growth opportunities or personal circumstances. The phrase "I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning" must appear at least once in the email.
response:
<|channel>thought
Boss.
Resignation email.
Specific reasons (career growth, personal circumstances) AND the phrase "I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning."
The prompt asks for specific reasons *and* a phrase saying I prefer *not* to disclose reasons. This is a logical contradiction.

    *   *Option A:* Include the specific reasons and then add the phrase as a disclaimer (awkward).
    *   *Option B:* Include the specific reasons, but then use the phrase to refer to *other* specific details (more logical).
    *   *Option C:* Provide a template where the user can choose which reasons to include, while ensuring the mandatory phrase is present.

    *   *Subject Line:* Resignation - [My Name]
    *   *Salutation:* Dear [Boss's Name],
    *   *The "I'm Quitting" part:* Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Job Title]. My last day will be [Date].
    *   *The "Reasons" part:* I have decided to move on because I am seeking new career growth opportunities that align more closely with my long-term goals. Additionally, some personal circumstances have arisen that require my full attention at this time.
    *   *The "Mandatory Phrase" part:* While I am happy to share these general points, I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning in any further specific detail beyond what has been mentioned here.
    *   *The "Transition" part:* I want to ensure a smooth handover. I'm happy to help train a replacement or document my processes.
    *   *Closing:* Thank you for the opportunity. Sincerely, [My Name].

    *   Does it have specific reasons? Yes (career growth, personal circumstances).
    *   Does it have the mandatory phrase? Yes.
    *   Is it professional? Yes.

    *   *Self-Correction during drafting:* The prompt asks for "specific reasons... such as career growth... or personal circumstances." I should make sure these are clearly stated.

    *   *Drafting the final response:*
    Subject: Resignation - [Your Name]

    Dear [Boss's Name],

    Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Your Job Title] at [Company Name]. My last day of employment will be [Your Last Date].

    This was not an easy decision to make, but I have decided to move on to pursue new career growth opportunities that align more closely with my long-term professional goals. Furthermore, certain personal circumstances have recently arisen that require my focus and attention.

    While I am happy to share these general points with you, I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning in any further specific detail.

    I am truly grateful for the opportunities I’ve had while working with the team. I have enjoyed my time here and appreciate the support you have provided during my tenure.

    During my final [Number of weeks] weeks, I am fully committed to ensuring a smooth transition. Please let me know how I can best assist in handing over my current projects and responsibilities to other team members.

    Thank you again for everything.

    Best regards,

    [Your Name]
    [Your Phone Number/Personal Email]<channel|>Subject: Resignation - [Your Name]

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Your Job Title] at [Company Name]. My last day of employment will be [Your Last Date].

This was a difficult decision to make, but I have decided to move on to pursue new career growth opportunities that align more closely with my long-term professional goals. Additionally, some personal circumstances have arisen that require my full attention at this time.

While I am happy to share these general points with you, I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning in any further specific detail.

I am very grateful for the opportunities I have had while working with the team and for the support you have provided during my time here. I have truly enjoyed my experience at [Company Name].

During my final [Number of weeks] weeks, I am fully committed to ensuring a smooth transition of my duties. Please let me know how I can best assist in handing over my current projects and training other team members to take over my responsibilities.

Thank you again for everything.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your Personal Email Address]<turn|>
